MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-18-2022 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-15-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Neoliminal'S 1) Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions 2) Motion That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 12/23/2021)
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. COLIN BARRENO, Esq. of the Law Office of Colin J. Barreno (Tel: 415-500-1644) for plaintiff;
Atty. JOSEPH BRAVO, Esq. of Bravo Law Offices (Tel: 415-860-4595) specially appearing with defendant SURAJ KUMAR RAJWANI.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Neoliminal's Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Mr. Bravo informs the court that he is substituting in as counsel for Mr. Rajwani and will be filing a substitution shortly.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows:

Plaintiff's motion is granted. Defendant is to provide full, complete, and code-complaint responses without objections within 10 days of the court's order.

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at rennepar85@gmail.com. The proposed order should include the amount of monetary sanctions against defendant and defendant's counsel of record (not Mr. Bravo) and the Court will either confirm or adjust the amount.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion For Order That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. COLIN BARRENO, Esq. of the Law Office of Colin J. Barreno (Tel: 415-500-1644) for plaintiff;
Atty. JOSEPH BRAVO, Esq. of Bravo Law Offices (Tel: 415-860-4595) specially appearing with defendant SURAJ KUMAR RAJWANI.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff Neoliminal's Motion For Order That Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Mr. Bravo informs the court that he is substituting in as counsel for Mr. Rajwani and will be filing a substitution shortly.

Matter argued and submitted.

Plaintiff's motion is granted. Defendant is to provide code-complaint responses without objections within 10 days of the court's order. Failure to provide code-compliant responses by this deadline will result in the Requests for Admission being deemed admitted and the defendant will be precluded from presenting contrary evidence at trial.

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at rennepar85@gmail.com. The proposed order should include the amount of monetary sanctions against defendant and defendant's counsel of record (not Mr. Bravo) and the Court will either confirm or adjust the amount.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
